commits.subject: issues API, slide-types API, model badge, nav→settings link
commits.body:
- API: GET /api/issues (admin, filter by status/severity/limit) and
  GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/issues/[id]. Lets admins triage agent reports
  without shelling into psql.
- API: GET /api/decks/[id]/slide-types returns global + deck-scoped
  types in one shot, with both id (UUID) and name (slug). Useful for
  the agent and for external scripts that don't want to stitch two
  sources together.
- Agent drawer header now shows a small model badge: host
  (LOCAL/API) + model name. Click to /settings. aiModel is plumbed
  from user prefs through the deck loader.
- Settings: API "metered" note only renders when an API model is
  selected — was redundant when the user is on a local Ollama model.
- Nav: BRUGER/NAME label is now a link to /settings on desktop, with
  hover state. Mobile retains the existing menu route.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: settings auto-save, editor menu reorg, model grouping, agent fix
commits.body:
- Settings: prefs form auto-submits on toggle / select change so the
  vim binding (and locale, model) actually persist without the user
  having to hunt for the Save button. Explicit Save still works.
- Settings → AI model: split into Local (Ollama) / API optgroups; new
  metered note under the dropdown clarifying API models cost per prompt.
- Deck editor action bar reorg: PRESENT | EXPORT | SHARE | more ▾ |
  AGENT. The theme picker entry and Delete deck moved into the new
  "more" dropdown. Outside-click closes it; transition:slide on open.
- Fix admin-captured issue (47f315e3…): slideType agent tools tried
  eq(slideTypes.id, $name) which Postgres rejected as a UUID cast.
  add_slide / patch_slide_type / delete_slide_type / inspect_slide_type
  now accept either UUID or name slug; lookup by name is scoped to
  global + this deck so we don't leak other decks' types. Tool
  descriptions updated.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: hover preview on slide-list rows
commits.body:
Hovering a row in the deck's left-hand slide list now floats a 360px
SlidePreview to the right of the row after a 220ms delay so quick
mouse passes don't trigger renders. The preview is anchored to the
row's vertical center and clamped to the viewport. Suppressed on
mobile (the slide list is full-screen there) and while a slide is
being dragged.

Only the hovered slide renders — we don't keep N preview iframes
alive in the list permanently.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Ctrl/Cmd-Z undo for deck-level actions
commits.body:
Adds an undo/redo stack to the deck editor covering the actions a user
is most likely to do by accident:

- Delete slide — re-creates with the same typeId + data, then PATCHes
  the deck's slideOrder to put the slide back at its original index.
- Add slide — deletes the newly created slide.
- Reorder slides — PATCHes slideOrder back to the previous array.
- Apply theme — PATCHes themeId back to the previous value.

Bindings: Cmd/Ctrl-Z to undo, Cmd/Ctrl-Shift-Z or Ctrl-Y to redo.
Inside an input/textarea/contenteditable we let the browser handle the
shortcut for native input-level undo, so typing history isn't shoved
into the deck stack. A small bottom-center toast confirms each action
("UNDONE: Delete slide" / "REDONE: …"). Stack is bounded at 50 entries
and any new action clears the redo side.

i18n: 7 new editor.* keys (da + en).
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: inline section number with label, sink × into group box
commits.body:
STField no longer reserves a 32px column for the section number — it
sits inline before the label. FieldEditor's group-typed list rows lose
the right-side column for ×: the button is now absolutely positioned
inside the group box's top-right corner. Group-field padding gets a
right gutter so the × doesn't overlap the inner inputs.

With both back, form column drops from 380–520 / 440–620 down to
340–420 / 380–480 (default / above 1440px), giving the preview area
back the space.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: redirect / to /decks for authenticated users
commits.body:
The root path had no page handler, so anyone signed in hitting
slidt.osogdata.dk/ directly got a 404. Anonymous users were already
fine — the layout redirects them to /login. Add a minimal root page
load that throws redirect(302, '/decks').
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: warn before navigating away with unsaved changes
commits.body:
New STUnsavedGuard component: hooks beforeNavigate to cancel in-app
navigations and show a fade/scale modal ("Stay" / "Leave anyway"), and
beforeunload to trigger the browser's native prompt on tab close /
hard reload.

Wired into:
- /templates/[id] — dirty when label / fields JSON / template HTML /
  CSS differ from last-saved snapshot. Snapshot refreshes on success.
- /themes/[id] — dirty when tokens / name / system prompt diverge.
- /settings — per-form dirty flags for profile and preferences,
  triggered on input/change and reset on successful submission.
  (Password form intentionally excluded.)

i18n: adds unsaved.title / body / stay / leave (da + en).
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: drop iframe allow-scripts, attach editors from parent
commits.body:
The previous setup used sandbox="allow-same-origin allow-scripts" plus
an in-iframe script to wire contenteditable. The browser warns that
those two flags together let an iframe escape its sandbox.

Switch to attaching contenteditable + key/blur listeners from the
parent window via iframe.contentDocument (allow-same-origin lets us
reach into the iframe DOM, and the parent script controls the
listeners). The injected EDITOR_STYLES still go into the rendered HTML
so hover/focus affordances work without parent script. onload re-runs
attachEditors after every render.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: widen form column to use horizontal space
commits.body:
Form column was a fixed 340px, so inputs in dense templates (friction's
sideA/sideB groups, principles' item lists) wrapped unnecessarily even
at desktop widths. Switch to minmax(380px, 520px) at default and
minmax(440px, 620px) above 1440px viewport, while keeping the slide
list narrower (260 → 240) to keep the preview area generous.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: list deck-scoped templates + admin promote-to-global
commits.body:
- /templates now loads global *and* deck-scoped templates (joined to
  decks for attribution). New scope filter chips (All / Global /
  Deck-scoped) with counts; deck-scoped cards show their parent deck.
- /templates/[id] gets a scope bar at the top: shows the current
  scope, the parent deck (if any), and a "Promote to global" button.
  Admin-only — non-admin users see a disabled button + hint. The
  promote action checks for a name collision against existing global
  templates and refuses with a 409 if it would collide.
- Deck editor type picker shows a small DECK badge on the preview of
  deck-scoped types so they're visually distinct from globals.
- Adds 11 new i18n keys (da + en) for the scope UI and promote flow.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: inline-edit list-item fields (one level of #each)
commits.body:
makeEditable now tracks #each list paths so fields inside iterations
get a data-slidt-field of "<list>.{{@index}}.<ref>" (or just
"<list>.{{@index}}" for {{fmt this}}). Handlebars renders {{@index}}
per iteration, giving each rendered element a unique path.

Also handles dotted top-level paths (e.g. {{fmt sideA.label}}) and
#each over a dotted source (e.g. {{#each sideA.body}}). Two-or-more
levels of nested #each remain un-tagged for now (would require
{{@../index}} chaining).

The deck editor's onedit handler walks the dotted path with an
immutable setDeep helper so list/group reactivity works, and it
tolerates malformed paths by leaving state untouched.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: double preview width on wide screens
commits.body:
At ≥1200px viewport, the template editor's right column grows from
380px to 760px so the live preview reads at a useful size. Below
that threshold the layout stays at 380px so the form column doesn't
collapse.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: inline-editable preview for top-level scalar fields
commits.body:
Click a title, eyebrow, kicker, etc. directly in the live preview to
edit it. Edits debounce-save through the same path as the form editor.

How it works:
- renderer.makeEditable wraps top-level {{fmt fieldName}} references with
  <span data-slidt-field="…"> markers. Fields inside #each blocks are
  skipped (no path to map them back); fields inside #if/#unless are
  still wrappable since context is unchanged.
- An EDITOR_SCRIPT is appended to the page body when render() is called
  with editable: true. It attaches contenteditable + focus/hover styling
  to marked elements and postMessages {type:'slidt:edit', field, value}
  back to the parent on blur or Enter.
- SlidePreview gains editable + onedit props. When editable, the iframe
  sandbox grants allow-scripts; the parent listens for messages from
  this iframe only and forwards edits.
- The deck editor passes editable={data.canEdit} and an onedit handler
  that updates slideDataMap[selectedSlideId] and triggers autosave.
- Lists and grouped fields stay form-only for now (deferred to a v2 that
  tracks per-item paths).
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: cross-route morph + dropdown/overlay transitions
commits.body:
- onNavigate hook in +layout.svelte uses the View Transitions API where
  available; deck list rows and the editor breadcrumb title share
  view-transition-name="deck-<id>" so clicking a deck morphs the row
  into the editor title. Falls back to a normal navigation on Safari.
- transition:slide on the More dropdown; transition:fade on the mobile
  menu; transition:fade backdrop + transition:scale picker for the
  type and theme overlays in the deck editor.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: plausible Danish dummy copy for template previews
commits.body:
Replace name-echo placeholders with field-name-aware sample copy so
template tiles (in the type picker and on /templates) read like real
slides. Lists with primitive items get three plausible bullets;
group-typed list items now produce three rows instead of two.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: stretch page-frame to fill shell height
commits.body:
Flex the page-shell so the framed area extends to the bottom of the
viewport instead of hugging short page content.
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: framed page shell, 60-day sessions, /templates grid + dummy previews
commits.body:
- Wrap authenticated content pages in a 1280px max-width frame with
  light-gray surround and subtle shadow; full-bleed routes (deck/theme
  editors, /share, /docs) opt out. Settings drops its own max-width since
  the global frame now provides it.
- resolveSession filters by expiresAt > now so stale tokens return null
  and the layout redirect to /login fires instead of leaking through into
  page loads (which produced a 404). SESSION_DURATION_SECONDS bumped to
  60 days and shared with both login routes' cookie maxAge.
- Type picker in the deck editor renders previews with buildDummyData so
  tiles show realistic placeholder content instead of empty fields.
- /templates is now a searchable card grid: server preloads a preset
  theme, page filters by label/name. Adds templates.search_placeholder
  and templates.empty_search i18n keys (da + en).
- Adds scripts/deploy.sh for ig-sub deploys (pull, rebuild app, healthz).
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: stop iframe inflating preview-frame height
commits.body:
The iframe declared width:1920px/height:1080px and used transform:scale()
to shrink visually, but transforms don't affect layout. The reserve div
added below the iframe meant the wrap grew to ~1080 + 1080*scale instead
of the intended ~1080*scale, so the framed border extended well past the
rendered slide. Positioning the iframe absolutely removes it from layout
flow; the reserve div alone now sizes the wrap.

commits.subject: validate inputs, accumulate text, add patch/delete_slide_type, broaden inspection
commits.body:
Findings from a real run (Flyttebussen deck): the agent shipped a deck
that 500s on export, with three slides whose data was JSON-stringified
instead of nested, and several list-of-string fields wrapped as
[{key:"…"}] objects. The final assistant message persisted as
"(tool calls only)" so the user couldn't see what the agent did. This
commit closes those holes.

Tooling:
- add_slide / patch_slide now validate the resolved data against the
  slide type's field schema (using the existing renderer/validate.ts).
  Validation errors are returned as the tool result so the agent sees
  them and can self-correct, instead of being discovered at render time.
- coerceObject() defensively JSON.parse's `data`/`patch` when Anthropic
  delivers them as a stringified blob (observed on deeply nested
  payloads). Stops "data stored as string" → silently empty slide.
- New patch_slide_type tool: update label/fields/htmlTemplate/css for
  deck-scoped types, with the same checkHandlebarsTemplate / checkCss
  guardrails create_slide_type uses. Undo restores the previous values.
- New delete_slide_type tool: deck-scoped only, refuses if any slide
  still references it. Undo recreates the type.
- inspect_slide_type now accepts an optional slideId so it can render
  with that slide's actual data, not just dummies. Closes the loop:
  create_slide_type → inspect (dummy) → add_slide → inspect (real).

Runner:
- finalText is no longer reset between iterations. The persisted
  assistant message now contains everything the user saw streaming
  across the whole turn, not only the last iteration's text.
- MAX_ITERATIONS 10 → 25. Real prompts ("build a deck from this URL")
  routinely hit the old cap with a few add_slide calls plus the new
  inspect cycles.

System prompt:
- Field-shape rules now include concrete WRONG/RIGHT examples for
  list-of-strings ([{item:"x"}] vs ["x"]) and list-of-groups, the two
  shapes the agent kept getting wrong.
- Visual verification rule extended: inspect after the FIRST add_slide
  of any slide type you haven't seen render before, with slideId so you
  see real content, not just dummies. Mention patch_slide_type and
  delete_slide_type as the iteration tools.
- Explicit "pass real JSON, never a stringified blob" line.

Tests: AGENT_TOOLS count 11 → 13 (patch_slide_type, delete_slide_type).

Implements the design-team handoff (design_handoff_slidt_swiss_terminal):
warm off-white + cobalt + ink, JetBrains Mono chrome, Neureal display,
3px ink rules, square corners, strict numbered grid, -_- as a system

Foundation:

- src/lib/i18n with t(key, params, locale) helper and a flat messages
  registry. Default locale 'da'; English alternates kept for future
  toggling. decksHeadline() handles Danish word-numerals.

Primitives (src/lib/components/st/):
- STBtn (ghost/primary/accent), STFace (idle/thinking/happy/sleep/alert),
  STField (numbered, schema-friendly), STTurn (transcript row),
  STMetaBar (pre-auth top bar), STNav (authenticated nav with indexed
  tabs + user/logout), STAgentDrawer (replaces AgentPanel — single-col
  transcript with parts[] interleaving, tool-call inline, image inline,
  cobalt undo chip, ⌘K toggle, bindable open prop).

Screens:
- Login: §01 — LOG IND, slidt + -_- hero, → FORTSÆT cobalt CTA.
- Decks list: 'X dæk i gang' (numeral-word) headline band, LAST WEEK
  agent-edit stat, table with hover, cobalt dot on most-recent.
- Empty state: hero with sleeping z_z mascot, 'Intet at sidde med endnu.'
- Editor: breadcrumb with GEMT pill + PRÆSENTÉR/EKSPORT.PDF/DEL/SLET DÆK
  / -_- ON · OFF, three-column body (slides 260px / form 340px / preview
  + agent), STField wrappers around FieldEditor, type-picker overlay.
- Themes/Templates/Share pages: chrome swept to tokens, contents kept.

Editor functional wiring (per spec section B):
- SAVED / GEMMER / FEJL pill from autosave state.
- Slide list rows show data-derived snippet (title or first text)
  primary, type label dimmed below; ⋮⋮ grip with optimistic local
  reorder so dropping snaps without waiting for the PATCH; cobalt
  drop indicator above/below the target depending on cursor half;
  delete (×) hover-revealed.
- Share dialog opens an overlay with the URL pre-selected and a copy
  button; auto-copy attempted, manual fallback when blocked.
- Delete deck via DELETE /api/decks/[id] with native confirm.
- -_- ON button toggles the agent drawer (bind:open).
- Tool-call chip + ↶ FORTRYD in the agent drawer footer; uses the
  existing undoPatch returned by agent tools.

Agent visual inspection:
- New tool inspect_slide_type(id): renders the slide type with dummy
  data via the existing renderer + Playwright at 1920×1080, returns
  PNG bytes. Tool result builds an Anthropic content block array with
  both image and text so the model literally sees the rendered slide.
- src/lib/server/screenshot.ts isolates the renderer + Playwright path.
- Runner emits image base64 over SSE; STAgentDrawer renders the
  thumbnail inline under the tool line.
- System prompt instructs to call inspect_slide_type after
  create_slide_type and iterate until the rendering looks right.
- agent-tools test count bumped 10 → 11.

src/lib/components/FieldEditor.svelte restyled to tokens; behaviour
unchanged. Decks loader queries agent_messages count (last 7d) for the
left-rail stat. Backwards-compat shims dropped — no fallback path needed.

of the ANTAL deck matches reference/ANTAL-Theta-slides.pdf page-by-page.

and generate.py; base-styles aligned with the reference typography,

.slide.dark/.light/.white theme variants, h1-h4 + em in deep-violet).

- src/renderer/symbols.ts: new. Loads corner wordmark + dandelion SVGs
  via Vite ?raw imports so the renderer runs in both server (PDF) and
  browser (SlidePreview) contexts without node:fs. Exports symbolDefs()
  + cornerMarkup() + dandelionSvg() used via a Handlebars helper.
- src/renderer/assets/: four logo SVGs bundled in src/ for ?raw.
- src/renderer/slide-wrap.ts: emits SVG corner via <use>.
- src/renderer/index.ts: prepends symbol defs to body.
- src/slide-types/*.ts: all 13 types ported to the reference's markup
  and scoped CSS.
- src/lib/server/font-css.ts: add Inter 500 for strong/.subheading.
- src/lib/components/SlidePreview.svelte: inject Inter @font-face
  alongside Neureal so web preview matches PDF output.
- static/fonts/inter/: Inter woff2 files for the preview iframe.

- scripts/import-deck-parse.ts: also accept reference's inline
  {type, ...inline-fields} slide shape, and auto-expand a top-level
  appendix array into a trailing appendix-list slide (mirrors
  generate.py's expand_appendix).
- scripts/import-deck.ts: default theme name matches seeded name.
- package.json: dev + CLI scripts use --env-file-if-exists=.env so

  of truth for future visual diffs.

commits.subject: Use script files for ralph commands and push progress after each task
commits.body:
Ralphify execs each command directly (no shell), so shell syntax like
if-then and pipes failed. Moved each command into a small executable
script under .ralph-commands/. Also added a final git-push step so
progress shows up on GitHub after each task.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Gitignore .env and fix ralph PATH in tmux session
commits.body:
Non-interactive tmux shells don't inherit ~/.local/bin from login
scripts, so the tmux command needs to prepend it explicitly for
ralph (pipx-installed) to be found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Route ralph through happy (-p --yolo) and add start-ralph.sh helper
commits.body:
Happy wraps claude with the same flags plus mobile-app visibility of
the running session. The start script sets IS_SANDBOX=1 so the
yolo flag is accepted under root, sets a 30-min per-iteration
timeout, and writes per-iteration logs under .ralph-logs/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Add RALPH.md to drive the autonomous implementation loop
commits.body:
Ralphify reads the frontmatter for agent command + commands whose
output gets interpolated into the prompt each iteration. Instructs
Claude to complete one Task per loop, verify expected output at each
step, and stop if anything deviates from the plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Add implementation roadmap and Plan 1 (core renderer + templates)
commits.body:
Roadmap decomposes the webservice into six subsystems that each
produce working, testable software on their own. Plan 1 covers the
foundation: a pure-TS render(deck, theme, templates) library, 13
built-in SlideTypes ported from generate.py, and the ANTAL-Theta
default theme extracted from styles.css. Later plans get written
once each previous subsystem lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Niels Ørbæk Chemnitz

commits.subject: Drop MinIO and agent budget from v1 per user preference
commits.body:
Local filesystem for assets (mounted data volume) keeps v1 ops simple
for a 7-user internal tool. Object storage moved to deferred v2+ for
when asset volume or multi-instance deploy justifies it. Rate
limiting removed — seven trusted users, cost logged for visibility
but no hard budget.

commits.subject: Add initial design spec for slidt slide webservice
commits.body:
Captures decisions from brainstorming: TS full-stack (SvelteKit +
Postgres), declarative slide-type templates (Handlebars + scoped CSS,
no code execution), form-driven editor with side-panel agent, Claude
agent with theme/content/new-template tools, email/password auth for
7 seeded users. Migration path from the existing ANTAL-Theta slides
pipeline included.
